text.title: Schema Markup Implementation
text.summary: JSON-LD schema patterns for Article, FAQ, Organization, and Breadcrumb types with validation workflow.

rule.statement: Validate all JSON-LD schema with Google Rich Results Test before deployment. Never deploy agent-generated schema without validation.
rule.rationale: Agents produce syntactically valid but semantically incorrect schema: wrong @type values, missing required fields, incorrect nesting. Invalid schema produces no rich results and may generate Search Console errors with no visible symptom.

list.item: 1. Generate schema from template or agent
2. Validate JSON syntax (jsonlint.com or IDE linter)
3. Test with Google Rich Results Test
4. Verify no conflicting schema definitions on the same page
5. Deploy and monitor Search Console for schema errors

rule.statement: Add schema markup in order of impact: Organization (global), Article (all blog posts), FAQPage (any Q&A content), BreadcrumbList (all pages with navigation hierarchy).
rule.rationale: Organization schema establishes brand entity globally. Article schema is required for Google Discover inclusion. FAQPage generates rich result expansion in SERPs. BreadcrumbList appears in search results and improves topic hierarchy signals.

list.item: Organization + Person: brand and author entity (global, add to header/footer)
Article/BlogPosting: all editorial content with author and datePublished
FAQPage: Q&A sections, "People Also Ask" style content
BreadcrumbList: all pages with 2+ level hierarchy

section.title: Required Fields by Schema Type

list.item: Article: headline, author (@type Person, name), publisher, datePublished, image
FAQPage: mainEntity array of Question + acceptedAnswer pairs
Organization: name, url, logo (ImageObject), sameAs array
BreadcrumbList: itemListElement array with ListItem, position, name, item (URL)

section.note: The last BreadcrumbList item (current page) should have a name but no item URL. Including a URL for the current page is technically valid but not required.
